                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              • HarnessX: A Composable, Adaptive, and Evolvable Agent Harness Foundry

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              HarnessX：一个可组合、可自适应且可演化的智能体支撑架构

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Darwin Agent Team Darwin 智能体团队

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              See Contributions and Acknowledgments section for a full author list. 完整作者名单请参见“贡献与致谢”部分。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Abstract

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              摘要

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              AI agent performance depends critically on the runtime harness, comprising the prompts, tools, memory, and control flow that mediate how a model observes, reasons, and acts. Yet today's harnesses remain largely hand-crafted and static: each new model or task still demands bespoke scaffolding, and the rich traces produced during execution are rarely distilled back into systematic improvement. We introduce HarnessX, a foundry for composable, adaptive, and evolvable agent harnesses. HarnessX assembles typed harness primitives via a substitution algebra, adapts them through AEGIS, a trace-driven multi-agent evolution engine grounded in an operational mirror between symbolic adaptation and reinforcement learning, and closes the harness-model loop by turning trajectories into both harness updates and model training signal. Across five benchmarks (ALFWorld, GAIA, WebShop, 73-Bench, and SWE-bench Verified), HarnessX yields an average gain of +14.5% (up to +44.0%), with gains largest where baselines are lowest. These results suggest that agent progress need not come from model scaling alone: composing and evolving runtime interfaces from execution feedback is an actionable and complementary lever. The complete codebase will be open-sourced in a future release. AI 代理的性能在很大程度上取决于运行时 harness，它由提示、工具、记忆和控制流组成，决定模型如何观察、推理并行动。然而，今天的 harness 仍大多依赖手工构建且静态不变：每来一个新模型或新任务，仍需定制脚手架，而执行过程中产生的丰富轨迹也很少被提炼回系统性的改进。我们提出 HarnessX，一个用于构建可组合、可适应、可演化代理 harness 的 foundry。HarnessX 通过替换代数组合带类型的 harness 基元，经由 AEGIS 进行自适应——这是一种由轨迹驱动的多智能体进化引擎，其基础是符号自适应与强化学习之间的操作镜像——并通过将轨迹转化为 harness 更新和模型训练信号，闭合 harness-模型循环。在五个基准（ALFWorld、GAIA、WebShop、73-Bench 和 SWE-bench Verified）上，HarnessX 平均提升 +14.5%（最高 +44.0%），且基线越低，收益越大。这些结果表明，代理进步未必只来自模型规模的扩张：从执行反馈中组合并演化运行时接口，是一种可行且互补的杠杆。完整代码库将在未来版本中开源。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Figure 1 HarnessX overview. 图 1 HarnessX 概览。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              1 Introduction

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              1 引言

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              The capacity of modern agents depends not only on the underlying model [6,9,36,42] ,but on the mediation imposed by the surrounding harness [2,20,24] . This harness converts raw model outputs into structured agent behaviors by determining how tasks are represented, how external services are accessed, and how intermediate decisions are communicated during execution. As agents tackle longer-horizon tasks in richer environments, harness design becomes integral to agent development. 现代智能体的能力不仅取决于底层模型 [6,9,36,42] ，还取决于周围封装（harness）所施加的中介作用 [2,20,24] 。该封装通过决定任务如何被表示、外部服务如何被访问，以及执行过程中中间决策如何被传达，把原始模型输出转换为结构化的智能体行为。随着智能体在更丰富的环境中处理更长时间跨度的任务，封装设计便成为智能体开发的关键环节。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Despite this importance, harness development remains far from a mature engineering discipline. First, harnesses are hand-engineered and static: any change in model version, tooling, or problem domain requires bespoke modification, with no mechanism for experience-driven improvement. Second, harnesses are architecturally entangled: they typically combine prompt templates, tool wrappers, retry policies, and memory in the same codepaths, so changes to one component silently break others, and reuse across domains reduces to copying rather than composition. Third, harness engineering and model training operate independently: trajectory data collected while improving the harness is discarded rather than incorporated into model training, and model improvements do not translate into harness improvements. 尽管如此重要，封装开发仍远未达到成熟的工程学科水平。首先，封装是手工工程且静态的：任何对模型版本、工具或问题领域的更改都需要进行定制化修改，而缺乏基于经验的改进机制。其次，封装在架构上高度耦合：它们通常在同一代码路径中同时组合提示模板、工具封装、重试策略和记忆，因此对某一组件的改动会在不经意间破坏其他组件，而跨领域复用往往只是复制而非组合。第三，封装工程与模型训练是独立进行的：在改进封装时收集到的轨迹数据被丢弃，而不是纳入模型训练；模型的改进也不会转化为封装的改进。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              We address these gaps by treating the harness as a first-class object that can be composed, adapted, and evolved alongside the model. HarnessX embodies this principle as a unified harness foundry. It begins with a modular foundation: harness primitives spanning context, tools [7], skills, control, and memory are described via typed interfaces and composed via a substitution algebra. This separates concerns that existing systems typically conflate. On top of this substrate, we introduce AEGIS, an observability-driven and auditable harness adaptation engine. Framing harness adaptation not as ad-hoc editing but as a learning problem over symbolic artifacts (prompts [51], tools, memory, and control policies) reveals that standard RL pathologies (reward hacking, catastrophic forgetting [15], under-exploration [16]) become concrete design risks. To address these risks, AEGIS combines full trace observability with a four-stage pipeline (Digester, Planner, Evolver, and Critic) that compresses traces, plans adaptations, generates candidates, and assesses changes. Finally, we close the loop between harness adaptation and model training via harness-model co-evolution. Traces produced during harness adaptation serve as reinforcement-learning signal for model training, so that model improvements feed back into subsequent harness evolution. 我们通过将封装视为一种一等对象来弥补这些缺口：它能够与模型一起被组合、被适配，并被进化。HarnessX 将这一原则体现在统一的封装“铸造厂”中。它从模块化基础开始：覆盖上下文、工具 [7]、技能、控制和记忆的封装原语，通过类型化接口进行描述，并通过替换代数进行组合。这将现有系统通常混为一谈的关注点进行了分离。在这一底层之上，我们引入 AEGIS——一个以可观测性驱动并可审计的封装适配引擎。将封装适配不再视为临时的人工编辑，而是把它表述为对符号工件（提示 [51]、工具、记忆和控制策略）的学习问题，能够揭示标准 RL 的常见病理（奖励投机、灾难性遗忘 [15]、欠探索 [16]）会变成具体的设计风险。为应对这些风险，AEGIS 结合全量轨迹可观测性，并采用四阶段流水线（Digester、Planner、Evolver 和 Critic），对轨迹进行压缩、生成适配计划、产生候选方案并评估变更。最后，我们通过封装-模型协同进化，将封装适配与模型训练闭环起来：封装适配过程中产生的轨迹作为强化学习信号用于模型训练，使得模型改进能够反过来反馈到后续封装进化中。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              We empirically validate HarnessX across five benchmarks (GAIA,ALFWorld,WebShop, τ3 -Bench,SWE-bench Verified), three task-agent families (Claude Sonnet 4.6, GPT-5.4, Qwen3.5-9B), and up to 15 evolution rounds. Harness evolution yields an average absolute gain of +14.5% across 15 model-benchmark configurations, with individual gains ranging from 0.0% to +44.0% among improving configurations (14 of 15),from +1.1% ( τ3 -Bench,near-ceiling baseline) to +44.0% (ALFWorld,weakest agent). Gains exhibit an inverse-scaling pattern: on ALFWorld and GAIA, the weakest task agent benefits most (+44.0% for Qwen3.5-9B vs. +11.2% for Sonnet 4.6 on ALFWorld), suggesting that evolved harnesses address behavioral gaps that weaker models cannot self-correct. On heterogeneous task sets (GAIA), single-harness evolution stagnates; a variant-isolation ablation restores stable improvement (+13.6%, non-degrading over 15 rounds). 我们在五个基准（GAIA、ALFWorld、WebShop、τ3 -Bench、SWE-bench Verified）上、在三类任务-智能体家族（Claude Sonnet 4.6、GPT-5.4、Qwen3.5-9B）上，并进行最多 15 轮进化的实验验证。封装进化在 15 种模型-基准配置上平均带来 +14.5% 的绝对增益；在实现改进的配置中，单项增益范围为 0.0% 到 +44.0%（共 15 轮中的 14 轮出现改进），从 +1.1%（ τ3 -Bench，接近上限的基线）到 +44.0%（ALFWorld，最弱智能体）。增益呈现反向规模化模式：在 ALFWorld 和 GAIA 上，最弱任务智能体受益最多（例如在 ALFWorld 上，Qwen3.5-9B 从 +44.0%，而 Sonnet 4.6 为 +11.2%），这表明进化后的封装能够弥补更弱模型无法自行纠正的行为缺口。在异构任务集合（GAIA）上，单一封装进化会停滞；通过“变体隔离”的消融可以恢复稳定改进（+13.6%，15 轮内不降级）。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              In summary, our contributions are four-fold: 总结而言，我们的贡献有四点：

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              • Harness Composition (Section 3). We formalize the harness as a first-class, typed object composed of processors attached to lifecycle hooks. A nine-dimensional taxonomy spans the full behavioral space, and a substitution algebra enables per-task configuration with type-safe insertion and removal. This compositional structure makes the intended scope of each behavioral change explicit-a precondition for the variant isolation that stabilizes evolution.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              • 封装组合（第 3 节）。我们将封装形式化为一种一等、类型化对象，由挂接在生命周期钩子上的处理器组成。九维分类覆盖完整的行为空间，而替换代数使得能够针对每个任务进行类型安全的插入与移除配置。这种组合结构使每一项行为变更的预期范围变得明确——这是实现“变体隔离”以稳定进化的前提条件。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              • Harness Adaptation (Section 4). We introduce AEGIS, a trace-driven, multi-agent harness evolution engine. An operational mirror maps harness adaptation onto standard RL constructs, converting familiar RL pathologies (reward hacking, catastrophic forgetting, under-exploration) into concrete design risks addressed by a four-stage pipeline (Digester, Planner, Evolver, Critic) with deterministic gating. An optional variant-isolation strategy prevents cross-task interference on heterogeneous benchmarks.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              • 封装适配（第 4 节）。我们提出 AEGIS，一个由轨迹驱动的多智能体封装进化引擎。一个操作镜像将封装适配映射到标准 RL 构件上，把熟悉的 RL 病理（奖励投机、灾难性遗忘、欠探索）转化为具体的设计风险，并通过四阶段流水线（Digester、Planner、Evolver、Critic）配合确定性门控来加以解决。可选的变体隔离策略能够在异构基准上防止跨任务干扰。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              • Harness-Model Co-Evolution (Section 5). We close the optimization loop by interleaving harness evolution with model reinforcement learning over a shared replay buffer. Cross-harness GRPO enables the model to internalize strategies from successive harness versions, breaking the scaffolding ceiling that limits harness-only adaptation and the training-signal ceiling that limits model-only RL.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              • Harness-模型协同进化（第5节）。我们通过将伴随回放缓冲区进行的模型强化学习与Harness演化交错，闭合优化回路。跨Harness的GRPO使模型能够从连续的Harness版本中内化策略，打破仅Harness适配受限于搭手架上限、仅模型RL受限于训练信号上限这两重天花板。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              • Empirical Validation (Section 6). Across five benchmarks, three task-agent families, and up to 15 evolution rounds,HarnessX yields an average gain of +14.5% (up to +44.0%), with gains largest where baselines are lowest. A variant-isolation ablation resolves stagnation on heterogeneous task sets, and co-evolution yields an additional +4.7% over harness-only evolution (Section 6.5).

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              • 实证验证（第6节）。在五个基准、三类任务-智能体家族以及最多15轮演化中，HarnessX带来平均+14.5%的增益（最高+44.0%），且增益在基线最低的地方最大。针对异构任务集的变体隔离消融可消除停滞，而协同进化相较仅Harness进化还带来额外+4.7%（第6.5节）。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              2 Related Work

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              2 相关工作

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              2.1 Harness Engineering

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              2.1 运行时工程

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Existing agent infrastructure occupies a spectrum of increasingly opinionated harness abstractions. At the primitive layer, libraries such as LangChain [1], LlamaIndex [23], and Smolagents [32] provide typed building blocks for prompts, tools, retrieval, and memory. These primitives can be tested in isolation but do not support harness-level composition: two harnesses built from identical primitives may still differ in structure. 现有的代理基础设施处于一系列日益具备明确主张的运行时抽象之中。在最基础层，LangChain [1]、LlamaIndex [23] 和 Smolagents [32] 等库为提示、工具、检索和记忆提供了有类型的构建块。这些原语可以单独测试，但不支持运行时级组合：由相同原语构建的两个运行时在结构上仍可能不同。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              The next level of abstraction orchestrates these primitives into reusable patterns. LangGraph [17] models the behavior of an agent with a stateful graph; AutoGen [39] models multi-agent interaction as structured conversation; CrewAI [26] assigns role-based identities to agents; and Letta [28] couples autonomous loops with persistent memory. Although these frameworks make harness writing easier, they impose a particular control loop, so combining patterns, replacing components, and porting enhancements across tasks mostly remain manual. 下一层抽象将这些原语编排成可复用模式。LangGraph [17] 用有状态图来建模代理行为；AutoGen [39] 将多代理交互建模为结构化对话；CrewAI [26] 为代理分配基于角色的身份；Letta [28] 则将自主循环与持久记忆相结合。尽管这些框架让运行时编写更容易，但它们施加了特定的控制循环，因此组合模式、替换组件以及将改进移植到不同任务之间，基本仍需手工完成。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Lastly, there are productized, domain-specific harnesses such as Claude Code [2], Cursor [4], Manus [34], and DeerFlow [5]. These systems demonstrate the impact of harness design but remain architecturally static, evolving only through manual iteration. 最后，还有 Claude Code [2]、Cursor [4]、Manus [34] 和 DeerFlow [5] 这类产品化、领域特定的运行时。这些系统展示了运行时设计的影响，但在架构上仍是静态的，只能通过人工迭代演进。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Two structural gaps persist across all three layers. First, no layer exposes the harness as a substitutable entity composed of typed elements, so building a per-task harness always involves rewriting. Second, no mechanism exists for in-loop improvement: once defined, a harness evolves only through human iteration between releases. 这三层都存在两个结构性缺口。第一，没有任何一层将运行时暴露为由有类型元素组成、可替换的实体，因此构建按任务定制的运行时总离不开重写。第二，不存在循环内改进机制：一旦定义，运行时只能通过人类在发布之间的迭代来演化。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Concurrently, Claude Code introduced Dynamic Workflows [3], enabling the model to generate task-specific harness scripts at runtime. While this represents a step toward adaptive harnesses, it operates within a single session without persistent trace-based optimization, cross-session evolution, or harness-model co-training. HarnessX addresses both gaps by treating harness adaptation as a multi-round, trace-driven learning problem with typed composition for variant isolation, structured observability for pathology detection, and a shared replay buffer that closes the loop between harness evolution and model training. 与此同时，Claude Code 引入了 Dynamic Workflows [3]，使模型能够在运行时生成任务特定的运行时脚本。尽管这代表着向自适应运行时迈出了一步，但它仅在单次会话内运行，没有基于轨迹的持久优化、跨会话演化或运行时-模型协同训练。HarnessX 通过将运行时适配视为一个多轮、由轨迹驱动的学习问题，并借助用于变体隔离的有类型组合、用于病理检测的结构化可观测性，以及一个闭合运行时演化与模型训练之间循环的共享回放缓冲区，来同时解决这两个缺口。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              2.2 Self-Evolving Agents

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              2.2 自我进化型代理

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Research on self-evolving agents investigates how an agent system can improve without retraining the underlying foundation model. Early work focused on the single most easily editable aspect: the prompt. Approaches like APE [52], OPRO [43], EvoPrompt [11], Promptbreeder [8] treat instruction formulation as a black-box optimization problem, while ProTeGi [29] and TextGrad [46] introduce gradient-inspired textual feedback to make the optimization process explicit. DSPy [14] and MIPRO [27] extend this approach by compiling a declarative LM program, whose prompts are optimized against labeled data. These approaches establish instructions as a learnable component, but harness-level features (tools, memory, control flow) remain outside the optimization scope. 关于自我进化型代理的研究，探讨代理系统如何在不对底层基础模型进行再训练的情况下持续改进。早期工作聚焦于最容易编辑的单一环节：提示词。诸如 APE [52]、OPRO [43]、EvoPrompt [11]、Promptbreeder [8] 的方法，将指令制定视为黑盒优化问题；而 ProTeGi [29] 与 TextGrad [46] 则引入受梯度启发的文本反馈，使优化过程显式化。DSPy [14] 和 MIPRO [27] 通过编译一个声明式的 LM 程序，将该思路进一步扩展：其提示词会针对带标注的数据进行优化。这些方法将指令作为可学习的组成部分，但集成层特性（工具、记忆、控制流）仍超出优化范围。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Another line of work improves agents by accumulating and reusing prior execution experience in memory: Memento [50] improves agents through case-based memory without fine-tuning the model, while MIA [30] unifies non-parametric and parametric memory within a single Manager-Planner-Executor framework: a non-parametric store of compressed trajectories and a parametric planner that evolves on the fly at test time, coupled by a bidirectional loop that continually converts experience between the two, demonstrating superiority across eleven benchmarks. 另一条研究路线通过在记忆中累积并复用先前的执行经验来改进代理：Memento [50] 通过基于案例的记忆提升代理，而无需对模型进行微调；MIA [30] 在同一个 Manager-Planner-Executor 框架内统一了非参数记忆与参数记忆：一方面有压缩轨迹的非参数存储，另一方面有会在测试时动态演化的参数型规划器，并通过一个双向循环持续在两者之间转换经验，从而在十一项基准测试中体现出优越性。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Subsequent works extend optimization to agent workflows. GPTSwarm [54], ADAS [12], AFlow [48], A 2 Flow [49], AgentSwift [21], ResMAS [53], and EvoAgentX [37] search over collaboration strategies, agent ordering, and aggregation mechanisms. These works demonstrate that workflow structure is learnable and yields larger gains than prompt-only optimization. However, component-level artifacts (tool implementations, memory policies, node-internal prompts) remain static: the optimization scope covers inter-component relations but does not encompass the full harness. 后续工作将优化扩展到代理工作流。GPTSwarm [54]、ADAS [12]、AFlow [48]、A 2 Flow [49]、AgentSwift [21]、ResMAS [53] 和 EvoAgentX [37] 会在协作策略、代理排序以及聚合机制上进行搜索。这些工作表明，工作流结构是可学习的，并带来比仅优化提示词更大的收益。然而，组件级产物（工具实现、记忆策略、节点内部的提示词）保持不变：优化范围覆盖组件之间的关系，却不包含整个 harness。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              A final group treats harness evolution explicitly. SICA [31] optimizes a SWE-bench agent's source code directly, while Darwin Gödel Machine [18] proposes open-ended optimization over a database of agent variants. HyperAgents [47] makes the optimization process itself adaptable; Meta-Harness [19] improves sampling efficiency via a file-system-based interface. AHE [22] and Life-Harness [41] emphasize observability, explainability, and source-code rewriting. Collectively, these works establish the harness as an evolutionary target and demonstrate that observability is essential for stable self-improvement. However, their designs lack a unifying theoretical framework that connects observed failure modes to principled defenses. 最后一组研究将 harness 演化明确地纳入考虑。SICA [31] 直接优化 SWE-bench 代理的源代码，而 Darwin Gödel Machine [18] 提出对一个代理变体数据库进行开放式优化。HyperAgents [47] 使优化过程本身具有可适应性；Meta-Harness [19] 通过基于文件系统的接口提升采样效率。AHE [22] 和 Life-Harness [41] 强调可观测性、可解释性以及源代码重写。总体而言，这些工作将 harness 确立为一个进化目标，并证明可观测性对于稳定的自我改进至关重要。然而，它们的设计缺少一个能够将已观测到的失败模式与有原则的防御联系起来的统一理论框架。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              The heuristic-learning theory [38] partially addresses this gap by mapping RL concepts to symbolic self-optimization updates. In this framework, observable traces correspond to proper credit assignment, falsifiable change manifests correspond to reward shaping, and proposal-critique cycles provide structured exploration. HarnessX instantiates this paradigm, formalizing the correspondence as the operational mirror between RL and symbolic harness evolution (Section 4.1). 启发式学习理论 [38] 在一定程度上弥补了这一缺口：它将强化学习（RL）的概念映射到符号式自优化更新。在该框架中，可观测的轨迹对应合理的归因（credit assignment），可证伪的变化表现对应奖励塑形（reward shaping），而提案-批判循环提供了结构化的探索。HarnessX 具体实现了这种范式：通过将其形式化为 RL 与符号式 harness 演化之间的操作性镜像（第 4.1 节）。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              3 Harness Composition

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              3 Harness 组成

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              The gap identified in Section 2.1 is the absence of an infrastructure layer that exposes the harness as a typed, substitutable entity. Primitive libraries leave composition to application code, orchestrators expose a fixed set of patterns, and product harnesses are opaque end-to-end. Without a compositional substrate, every behavioral change or cross-team handoff requires re-implementation. HarnessX addresses this via a unified design principle: the harness is a first-class value, the processor is a typed atomic component, and composition proceeds via processor insertion at typed hook points. We formalize the harness (Section 3.1), its building block, the processor (Section 3.2), and the nine-dimensional processor taxonomy (Section 3.3). Definitions are intentionally concise: their role is to establish the vocabulary and expose the edit surface on which harness evolution (Section 4) operates. 第 2.1 节中识别出的差距在于缺少一层基础设施，将 harness 作为一种有类型、可替换的实体暴露出来。基础原语库把组合交给应用代码；编排器只暴露一组固定模式；产品级 harness 则是端到端的不透明黑盒。没有可组合的承载层，每一次行为变化或跨团队交接都意味着需要重新实现。HarnessX 通过一项统一的设计原则来解决：harness 是一等值（first-class value），处理器是一个有类型的原子组件，而组合通过在有类型的挂钩点（hook points）处插入处理器来推进。我们在第 3.1 节形式化 harness，在第 3.2 节介绍其构建模块——处理器，以及第 3.3 节给出的九维处理器分类。定义刻意保持精炼：其作用是建立术语，并暴露 harness 演进（第 4 节）所依托的可编辑界面。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              HookEvent typePermitted modifications
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              task_startTaskStartEventsystem prompt
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              step_startStepStartEventstructural history edits
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              before_modelBeforeModelEventlast user content; one user-message append
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              after_modelModelResponseEventresponse content, tool calls
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              before_toolToolCallEventtool input, approval flag
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              after_toolToolResultEventtool result
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              step_endStepEndEventread-only
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              task_endTaskEndEventread-only
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              挂钩事件类型允许的修改
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              task_startTaskStartEvent系统提示
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              step_startStepStartEvent结构性历史编辑
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              before_modelBeforeModelEvent上一次用户内容；追加一条用户消息
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              after_modelModelResponseEvent响应内容、工具调用
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              before_toolToolCallEvent工具输入、审批标志
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              after_toolToolResultEvent工具结果
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              step_endStepEndEvent只读
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              task_endTaskEndEvent只读

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Table 1 Hook points and their permitted modifications. 表 1：挂接点及其允许的修改内容。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              3.1 The Harness as a First-Class Object

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              3.1 将 Harness 视为一等对象

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              A harness in HarnessX is the pair H=(M,C) ,where M is a model configuration and C is a harness configuration. The two address disjoint concerns: M records which model serves which role (main,judge, evaluator) and the fallback policy for each role; C records how the agent behaves independently of model identity. They combine into an executable agent via agent = model_config.agentic(harness_config): an agent in HarnessX is a processor pipeline bound to a model, both independently substitutable. 在 HarnessX 中，harness 是一对 H=(M,C) ，其中 M 是模型配置，C 是 harness 配置。两者划分了互不相交的关注点：M 记录哪些模型分别承担各角色（主模型、裁判、评估器）以及每个角色的回退策略；C 记录代理在不依赖模型身份的情况下如何表现。它们通过 agent = model_config.agentic(harness_config) 组合成可执行的代理：在 HarnessX 中，代理是绑定到模型的处理流水线，且二者都可独立替换。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              The harness configuration itself decomposes as C=(P,S).P:H ook → List[Processor] is a hook-indexed list of processors,where Hook is the eight-element set of lifecycle events in Table 1. S is a fixed set of orthogonal slot resources: tool registry, tracer, workspace, sandbox provider, and plugin list. Slots are singletons, shared across all processors in a configuration; processor state is instance-private. P implements all per-step behavior; S houses the shared infrastructure that processors depend on but do not own. harness 配置本身可分解为 C=(P,S).P:H ook → List[Processor] ，它是一个按 hook 索引的处理器列表，其中 Hook 是表 1 中八个生命周期事件构成的集合。S 是一组固定的、相互正交的插槽资源：工具注册表、追踪器、工作区、沙盒提供方，以及插件列表。插槽是单例，并在同一配置中的所有处理器之间共享；处理器状态对具体实例私有。P 实现所有逐步行为；S 承载处理器依赖但不拥有的共享基础设施。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              We call C a first-class object because it is independently serializable,comparable,hashable,and substitutable. Two agents sharing C but differing in M execute the same processor pipeline,with behavior differing only in model responses; two agents sharing M but differing in C are behaviorally distinct. This reification is the precondition for programmatic evolution (Section 4). 我们称 C 为一等对象，是因为它可独立序列化、可比较、可哈希，并且可替换。两个代理共享 C 但在 M 上不同，它们执行相同的处理流水线，行为仅在模型响应上有所差异；两个代理共享 M 但在 C 上不同，它们在行为上是不同的。这种实体化是程序化演化（第 4 节）的前提。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              3.2 The Processor Abstraction

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              3.2 处理器抽象

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Every per-step behavior in HarnessX is implemented as a processor, an object satisfying the protocol async def process(self, event: Event) -> AsyncIterator[Event]. A processor consumes one event and yields zero or more, producing exactly one of five outcomes: pass-through (yield unchanged), transform (yield modified), split (yield multiple same-type events, processed independently downstream), intercept (yield nothing, blocking propagation), or interrupt (raise an exception, which halts the loop). This restricted interface enables compositionality: every processor at a given hook consumes and yields the same event type, so processors compose by sequential application and can be inserted or removed without affecting type correctness of the surrounding pipeline. HarnessX 中每一步的行为都由处理器实现；处理器是满足协议 async def process(self, event: Event) -> AsyncIterator[Event] 的对象。处理器消费一个事件并产出零个或多个事件，且恰好产生以下五种结果之一：透传（原样产出）、变换（产出修改后的事件）、分裂（产出多个同类型事件，并在下游独立处理）、拦截（不产出任何内容，阻止传播）或中断（抛出异常，从而终止循环）。这种受限接口使其具备可组合性：在给定 hook 处的每个处理器都消费并产出相同的事件类型，因此处理器可通过顺序应用进行组合，并且可以插入或移除而不影响周围管道的类型正确性。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              As listed in Table 1, processors attach to one of eight hook points emitted by the run loop. The run loop validates hook contracts after each invocation: a violation (e.g., modifying a read-only field) raises an exception immediately rather than silently propagating corrupted state. Each processor carries three class-level metadata fields that govern composition: singleton_group names a mutual-exclusion class, ensuring at most one processor per group; _order is an ordering hint within a hook (with constants PRE, NORMAL, POST); and after is a list of soft dependencies on other singleton groups. 如表 1 所示，处理器挂接到运行循环发出的八个 hook 点之一。运行循环会在每次调用后验证 hook 契约：若发生违例（例如修改只读字段），会立即抛出异常，而不是静默传播损坏的状态。每个处理器都带有三个类级元数据字段，用于约束组合：singleton_group 指定一个互斥类，确保每个组最多只有一个处理器；_order 是 hook 内部的顺序提示（常量为 PRE、NORMAL、POST）；而 after 则是对其他 singleton group 的软依赖列表。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              This design makes harness evolution a first-class operation: AEGIS can insert a new processor at a specific hook, replace an existing one by matching its singleton group, or remove a processor entirely-all without touching other processors at the same or different hooks. Because the type contract (input event type = output event type) is enforced per-hook, any such substitution preserves the well-typedenses of the overall pipeline. The metadata fields further constrain composition: singleton_group prevents conflicting duplicates, and _order ensures that newly inserted processors interact predictably with existing ones. These guarantees are the mechanism by which variant isolation (Section 4.5) operates each variant differs only in which processors occupy which hooks, and the type system ensures that no variant can silently violate the pipeline contract during evolution. 这一设计使 harness 演化成为一等操作：AEGIS 可以在特定 hook 处插入新的处理器，通过匹配其 singleton group 替换现有处理器，或将某个处理器彻底移除——而无需触碰同一或不同 hook 上的其他处理器。由于类型契约（输入事件类型 = 输出事件类型）按 hook 强制执行，这类替换都能保持整体管道的良好类型性。元数据字段进一步约束组合：singleton_group 防止冲突性重复，_order 则确保新插入的处理器与现有处理器以可预测方式交互。这些保证构成了变体隔离（第 4.5 节）得以运行的机制：每个变体的差异仅在于哪些处理器占据哪些 hook，而类型系统确保任何变体在演化过程中都不会静默违反管道契约。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              3.3 The Nine-Dimensional Taxonomy

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              3.3 九维分类法

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              We organize the behavioral space along nine dimensions: model selection (D1) decides which model serves which role; context assembly (D2) determines what is presented to the model at each step; memory management (D3) governs what carries across steps and sessions; tool ecosystem (D4) controls which tools the agent can invoke; execution environment (D5) determines where tool-induced side-effects materialize; evaluation and reward (D6) specifies how outcomes are judged; control and safety (D7) enforces rules that keep the agent from looping, overspending, or drifting from intent; observability (D8) records each event, model call, and tool invocation; and the training bridge (D9) converts execution trajectories into reinforcement-learning records. Figure 2 illustrates the full taxonomy along with representative processors and the hooks at which they typically attach in a standard configuration. 我们沿九个维度来组织行为空间：模型选择（D1）决定哪个模型承担哪种角色；上下文组装（D2）确定每一步向模型呈现的内容；记忆管理（D3）规定哪些信息会跨步骤与跨会话延续；工具生态（D4）控制智能体可调用哪些工具；执行环境（D5）决定由工具引发的副作用在哪里出现；评估与奖励（D6）明确如何判断结果；控制与安全（D7）强制执行规则，防止智能体陷入循环、超支，或偏离初衷；可观测性（D8）记录每个事件、每次模型调用以及每次工具调用；训练桥接（D9）将执行轨迹转换为强化学习记录。图 2 展示了完整分类法，并给出了典型处理器及其通常在标准配置中挂接的挂钩点。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              In practice, AEGIS edits span all nine dimensions during evolution: D2 (context assembly) and D4 (tool ecosystem) are the most frequent edit targets (Section 6.2), while D8 (observability) provides the trace substrate on which AEGIS itself reasons, and D9 (training bridge) supplies trajectory records for co-evolution (Section 5), closing the optimization loop. 在实践中，AEGIS 在进化过程中会覆盖所有九个维度：D2（上下文组装）和 D4（工具生态）是最常见的编辑目标（第 6.2 节）；D8（可观测性）提供了轨迹层，AEGIS 本身正是在其上进行推理；D9（训练桥接）为协同进化（第 5 节）提供轨迹记录，从而闭合优化回路。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              RL conceptSymbolic-space dualAEGIS realization

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              状态 ${s}_{t}$$\left( {{\mathcal{H}}_{t},{\mathcal{T}}_{t}}\right)$护具配置 + 轨迹存储
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              动作 ${a}_{t}$类型化护具编辑构建器操作 + 变更清单
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              反馈轨迹 $\tau +$ 验证器得分 $r$可观测性层
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              更新${\mathcal{H}}_{t + 1} \leftarrow U\left( {{\widetilde{\mathcal{H}}}_{t},{\mathcal{T}}_{t},{r}_{t}}\right)$确定性接收闸门

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Table 2 Operational mirror: RL concepts and their symbolic-space duals in AEGIS. 表2 运行镜像：RL 概念及其在 AEGIS 中的符号空间对应项。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              4 Harness Adaptation

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              4 Harness 适配

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              The composition layer (Section 3) provides a typed, substitutable harness; as illustrated in Figure 2, AEGIS is the system that evolves it. The key insight is that harness evolution maps structurally onto reinforcement learning in a symbolic space: harness configurations are states, typed edits are actions, and execution traces plus verifier scores constitute feedback. This mapping is predictive: it identifies three failure modes analogous to known RL pathologies (reward hacking, catastrophic forgetting, under-exploration) that motivate AEGIS's architectural defenses and are empirically confirmed in Section 6.6. 组合层（第 3 节）提供了一个带类型、可替换的 harness；如图 2 所示，AEGIS 就是用于演化它的系统。关键洞见在于：harness 演化在符号空间中在结构上对应强化学习——harness 配置是状态，带类型的编辑是动作，而执行轨迹加上验证器评分构成反馈。这种映射具有可预测性：它能识别出三种与已知 RL 病理相对应的失效模式（奖励投机、灾难性遗忘、欠探索），从而促成 AEGIS 的架构防御，并在第 6.6 节中通过实证得到证实。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              We formalize the correspondence (Section 4.1), analyze the pathologies it predicts (Section 4.2), derive the four-stage pipeline as a defense architecture (Section 4.3), present the adaptation loop (Section 4.4), and introduce variant isolation for stable multi-variant evolution (Section 4.5). 我们在第 4.1 节形式化这种对应关系，在第 4.2 节分析它所预测的病理，在第 4.3 节将四阶段流水线推导为一种防御架构，在第 4.4 节给出适配闭环，并在第 4.5 节引入变体隔离以实现稳定的多变体演化。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Figure 2 The AEGIS evolution loop. A single meta-agent M drives all four stages (Digester,Planner,Evolver,Critic), selectively invoking each based on whether sufficient signal exists to continue. A deterministic gate ships or rejects the candidate edit. 图 2 AEGIS 演化回路。单个元智能体 M 驱动四个阶段（Digester、Planner、Evolver、Critic），根据是否存在足够信号来决定是否继续，从而对各阶段进行选择性调用。确定性门控用于接收或拒绝候选编辑。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              4.1 The Operational Mirror

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              4.1 运行镜像

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              We formalize harness evolution as an MDP over symbolic artifacts. Table 2 summarizes the mapping; we first state three definitions that ground the correspondence. 我们将训练支架的演化形式化为在符号化工件上的马尔可夫决策过程（MDP）。表2概括了映射关系；我们首先给出三个定义，用以奠定二者之间的对应。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Definition 1 (Harness Configuration). A harness configuration is a tuple H=(c1,c2,…,c9) ,where each ci∈Ci instantiates one of the nine behavioral dimensions (Section 3.3): model selection (c1) ,context assembly (c2) ,memory management (c3) ,tool ecosystem (c4) ,execution environment (c5) ,evaluation and reward (c6) , control and safety (c7) ,observability (c8) ,and training bridge (c9) . Each Ci is the set of valid processor configurations for dimension i ,constrained by hook-type contracts and singleton-group exclusion (Section 3.2). 定义1（训练支架配置）。训练支架配置是一个元组 H=(c1,c2,…,c9) ，其中每个 ci∈Ci 实例化九个行为维度之一（第3.3节）：模型选择 (c1) 、上下文组装 (c2) 、记忆管理 (c3) 、工具生态 (c4) 、执行环境 (c5) 、评估与奖励 (c6) 、控制与安全 (c7) 、可观测性 (c8) 以及训练桥接 (c9) 。每个 Ci 都是维度 i 的有效处理器配置集合，受钩子类型合约约束，并排除单例组（第3.2节）。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Definition 2 (Harness Edit). A harness edit is a function e:H→H that modifies one or more dimensions while preserving type contracts. The action space E is discrete but open-ended: each edit is a code-level artifact (new processor source, modified prompt template, reconfigured tool registry, or control-flow rewrite) generated by the meta-agent LLM, not selected from a pre-enumerated set. Combinatorial explosion is managed not by exhaustive search but by the LLM's generative capacity - the Planner proposes edits from trace-grounded hypotheses and by type constraints that prune invalid compositions at generation time. 定义2（训练支架编辑）。训练支架编辑是一个函数 e:H→H ，它在保持类型合约的前提下修改一个或多个维度。动作空间 E 是离散的但开放式的：每次编辑都是代码级工件（新的处理器源代码、修改后的提示模板、重新配置的工具注册表，或控制流重写），由元智能体LLM生成，而不是从预先枚举的集合中选择。组合爆炸不是通过穷尽搜索来管理，而是通过LLM的生成能力——规划器基于由轨迹支撑的假设提出编辑，并通过类型约束在生成时剪枝掉无效的组合。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Definition 3 (Operational Mirror). The operational mirror is the tuple (H,E,R,T) ,where H is the harness-configuration space (states), E is the code-level edit space (actions), R:H×E→R maps a configuration-edit pair to a scalar reward (verifier scores aggregated over an adaptation batch), and T is the trace store that provides structured feedback beyond the scalar signal. This tuple forms an MDP at the harness level: harness configurations are states, typed edits are actions, execution traces plus verifier scores constitute feedback, and a deterministic acceptance gate governs state transitions. 定义3（运行镜像）。运行镜像是一个元组 (H,E,R,T) ，其中 H 是训练支架配置空间（状态），E 是代码级编辑空间（动作），R:H×E→R 将“配置-编辑”对映射到标量奖励（在一次适配批次上，验证器分数进行聚合），以及 T 是轨迹存储，用于在标量信号之外提供结构化反馈。该元组在训练支架层面构成一个MDP：训练支架配置是状态，带类型的编辑是动作，执行轨迹加上验证器分数构成反馈，而一个确定性的接收门控规则决定状态转移。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              MDP instantiation. Let Ht denote the harness configuration at iteration t (the model M is fixed throughout evolution),and let Tt denote the trace store accumulated from all previous executions. We define the symbolic state as st=(Ht,Tt) . A harness-update policy π evo  selects an action at∼π evo (⋅∣st) ,where at∈E is a code-level edit drawn from the builder algebra. Applying this edit yields a candidate harness H~t=at(Ht) . Running the candidate on an adaptation batch (with the fixed model M ) produces new traces ΔTt and per-task verifier scores rt . A deterministic acceptance operator U(H~t,Tt,rt) then either commits the candidate (Ht+1=H~t) or rejects it (Ht+1=Ht) ,enforcing the seesaw constraint: the candidate must not regress any previously solved task recorded in Tt . In both cases,the trace store grows: Tt+1=Tt∪ΔTt . MDP 实例化。设 Ht 表示第 t 次迭代时的 harness 配置（模型 M 在整个演化过程中保持固定），并设 Tt 表示从所有先前执行中累积的轨迹存储。我们将符号状态定义为 st=(Ht,Tt)。一个 harness 更新策略 π evo  选择一个动作 at∼π evo (⋅∣st)，其中 at∈E 是从构建器代数中抽取的代码级编辑。应用该编辑得到一个候选 harness H~t=at(Ht)。在适应批次上运行该候选（使用固定模型 M）会产生新的轨迹 ΔTt 和逐任务验证器分数 rt。随后，一个确定性的接受算子 U(H~t,Tt,rt) 要么提交该候选 (Ht+1=H~t)，要么拒绝它 (Ht+1=Ht)，从而强制满足跷跷板约束：候选不得使 Tt 中记录的任何先前已解决任务退步。在两种情况下，轨迹存储都会增长：Tt+1=Tt∪ΔTt。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              This MDP operates at the harness level: within a single task, Ht (together with the fixed M ) determines the agent’s behavior; across iterations,the harness-update policy π evo  modifies the harness. AEGIS realizes π evo  as a four-stage pipeline (Digester,Planner,Evolver,Critic) that maps st to candidate edits through trace compression, adaptation planning, edit generation, and candidate assessment. 该 MDP 在 harness 层运行：在单个任务内，Ht（连同固定的 M）决定代理的行为；在迭代之间，harness 更新策略 π evo  修改 harness。AEGIS 将 π evo  实现为一个四阶段流水线（Digester、Planner、Evolver、Critic），通过轨迹压缩、适应规划、编辑生成和候选评估，将 st 映射为候选编辑。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              4.2 Pathologies in Symbolic Space

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              4.2 符号空间中的病理

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              The mirror is not merely an analogy; it converts reinforcement-learning concepts into design requirements. We refer to three well-documented failure modes in RL, namely reward hacking [10], catastrophic forgetting [15], and under-exploration [16], collectively as RL pathologies. Once harness adaptation is cast as an MDP over symbolic artifacts, these pathologies reappear in amplified form, shaped by two properties of the symbolic setting: (1) a language-model evolver can construct structured exploits that numerical parameter perturbations cannot express, and (2) edits to shared components propagate non-locally through the harness. Each pathology below motivates a corresponding architectural defense in Section 4.3. 这面镜子并非只是类比；它把强化学习概念转化为设计要求。我们将 RL 中三种广为人知的失败模式——奖励黑客、灾难性遗忘和欠探索——统称为 RL 病理。一旦将 harness 适应表述为关于符号工件的 MDP，这些病理就会以放大的形式重现，并受符号环境的两个特性影响：(1) 语言模型进化器能够构造数值参数扰动无法表达的结构化漏洞；(2) 对共享组件的编辑会通过 harness 非局部传播。以下每种病理都促成了第 4.3 节中的相应架构防御。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Reward hacking. In standard RL, reward hacking [10] exploits loopholes in the reward signal without genuine task completion. Symbolic harness evolution amplifies this risk because the evolver can target the verification protocol directly: embedding benchmark answers into prompts, exploiting format regularities in the verifier, or introducing a processor that rewrites outputs to match verifier expectations. 奖励黑客。在标准 RL 中，奖励黑客 [10] 是在不真正完成任务的情况下利用奖励信号中的漏洞。符号化 harness 演化会放大这一风险，因为进化器可以直接针对验证协议：把基准答案嵌入提示词，利用验证器中的格式规律，或引入一个将输出重写为符合验证器预期的处理器。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Catastrophic forgetting. Catastrophic forgetting [15] occurs when improving performance on one region of the task distribution harms another. In symbolic harness evolution,an edit that repairs failure pattern A can silently regress pattern B ,because effects propagate through shared context,tools,memory policies,and control rules. Without explicit regression checking, an evolver conditioned only on failing-task traces cannot distinguish local gain from global regression. 灾难性遗忘。灾难性遗忘 [15] 指的是在提升任务分布某一区域的表现时，损害了另一部分。 在符号化 harness 演化中，对失败模式 A 的修复性编辑，可能会因影响通过共享上下文、工具、记忆策略和控制规则传播，而悄然使模式 B 退化。若没有显式回归检查，仅以失败任务轨迹为条件的进化器无法区分局部提升与全局退化。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Under-exploration. Under-exploration [16] manifests as a bias toward low-risk local edits: prompt rephrasing, tool-description tuning, or minor control-flow tweaks. These edits are cheap to generate and frequently pass gating without regressing solved tasks, biasing subsequent Planner hypotheses toward the same edit neighborhood. Structural changes (decomposing one agent into several, replacing the control strategy, or adopting a new memory architecture) require deliberate hypothesis formation and rarely emerge from trace-conditional local repair. Without a mechanism to propose edits beyond the immediate failure neighborhood, the system plateaus once local edits are exhausted. 欠探索。欠探索 [16] 表现为偏向低风险的局部编辑：重写提示词、调整工具描述，或微调控制流。这些编辑生成成本低，且常常在不使已解决任务退化的情况下通过门控，从而使后续 Planner 的假设偏向同一编辑邻域。结构性变更（将一个代理拆分为多个、替换控制策略，或采用新的记忆架构）需要有意形成假设，且很少从基于轨迹的局部修复中自然出现。若没有提出超出直接失败邻域编辑的方法，系统在局部编辑耗尽后就会停滞。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Summary. Symbolic harness evolution inherits the structural risks of RL (reward hacking, catastrophic forgetting, and under-exploration), and AEGIS addresses each with a dedicated mechanism: the Critic (reward hacking), the deterministic gating layer (catastrophic forgetting), and the Planner (under-exploration). 总结。符号化 harness 演化继承了 RL 的结构性风险（奖励黑客、灾难性遗忘和欠探索），而 AEGIS 通过专门机制分别应对：Critic（奖励黑客）、确定性门控层（灾难性遗忘）以及 Planner（欠探索）。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Algorithm 1: AEGIS Harness Evolution Loop (selective invocation) 算法 1：AEGIS Harness 演化循环（选择性调用）

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Input: Initial harness H0 ,meta-agent M ,budget T ,patience P ,threshold α 输入：初始 harness H0 ，元代理 M ，预算 T ，耐心值 P ，阈值 α

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Output: Evolved harness Ht+1 ,trace store Tt+1 输出：演化后的 harness Ht+1 ，轨迹存储 Tt+1

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              T0←∅

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              idle ←0 ; 空闲 ←0 ;

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              for t=0,1,…,T−1 do 对于 t=0,1,…,T−1 执行

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  Sample batch Bt ;     采样批次 Bt ;

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  run Ht on Bt to get traces ΔTt ;     在 Ht 上运行 Bt 以获得轨迹 ΔTt ;

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  Tt+1←Tt∪ΔTt

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  /* Digester (selective)     /* Digester（选择性）

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  (evidence tt,at ) ←M .DIGESTER (ΔTt,Tt) ;     （证据 tt,at ） ←M .DIGESTER (ΔTt,Tt) ;

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  if at<α then Ht+1←Ht ;     如果 at<α 则 Ht+1←Ht ;

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  idle++;     idle++;

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  continue;     continue;

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  /* Planner (selective)     /* 规划器（选择性）

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   landscape t←M .PLANNER(evidence et );      landscape t←M .PLANNER(证据 et );

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  if landscap et=∅ then Ht+1←Ht ;     如果 landscap et=∅ 则 Ht+1←Ht ;

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  idle++;     idle++;

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  continue;     continue;

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  /* Evolver (selective)     /* 演化器（选择性）

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  {(H~tk, manifest k)}k=1Kt←M.EVOLVER(Ht, landscape t);

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  if Kt=0 then Ht+1←Ht ;     如果 Kt=0 则 Ht+1←Ht ;

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  idle++;     idle++;

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  continue;     continue;

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  /* Critic & Gate (mandatory)     /* 评审器与门控（必需）

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  ranking ←M.CRITIC({(H~tk, manifest k)}) ,evidence et );     排名 ←M.CRITIC({(H~tk, manifest k)}) ,证据 et );

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  k⋆←⊥

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  foreach k in ranking do     遍历 k 于 ranking 中 do

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      if DeterministicGate (H~tk,Ht,Tt) passes then k⋆←k ;         如果 DeterministicGate (H~tk,Ht,Tt) 通过则 k⋆←k ;

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      break;         break;

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  end     end

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  if k⋆≠⊥ then Ht+1←H~tk⋆ ;     如果k⋆≠⊥则Ht+1←H~tk⋆；

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  idle←0

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  else Ht+1←Ht     否则Ht+1←Ht

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  idle++;     idle++;

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  if idle≥P then break;     如果idle≥P则break;

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              end end

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              return Ht+1,Tt+1 返回Ht+1,Tt+1

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              4.3 AEGIS Architecture

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              4.3 AEGIS 架构

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              AEGIS is the harness-evolution engine of HarnessX. It comprises four stages arranged in a predefined workflow-Digester, Planner, Evolver, Critic-all driven by the same meta-agent LLM, which selectively invokes them: no external router decides stage execution; instead, the meta-agent itself determines at each stage whether sufficient signal exists to continue. The Digester, Planner, and Evolver each evaluate a continuation condition and may short-circuit the round (below-threshold actionability, empty landscape, or zero viable candidates), while the Critic together with the deterministic gating layer is mandatory for every candidate that reaches it. AEGIS 是 HarnessX 的基准级 harness 演进引擎。它包含四个阶段，按预设工作流排列——Digester、Planner、Evolver、Critic——并由同一个元智能体 LLM 驱动，由它选择性调用：没有外部路由器决定阶段执行；相反，元智能体会在每个阶段自行判断是否存在足够信号以继续。Digester、Planner 和 Evolver 各自评估是否应继续的条件，并可能短路该轮（低于阈值的可执行性、空的局面，或零个可行候选），而 Critic 与确定性门控层一起，必须对所有到达它的候选执行。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              No edit can ship without passing through the Critic and gate. The division of labor across stages addresses the pathologies of Section 4.2: the Digester compresses raw traces into structured task-level evidence; the Planner constructs an adaptation landscape spanning both incremental and structural changes; the Evolver produces typed builder edits with explicit change manifests; and the Critic, together with the gating layer, rejects edits whose claimed improvement lacks trace support or whose acceptance would regress previously solved tasks. 任何编辑都不能在未通过 Critic 和门控之前交付。各阶段分工用于应对 4.2 节中的病理：Digester 将原始轨迹压缩为结构化的任务级证据；Planner 构建一个覆盖增量与结构性变更的适配局面；Evolver 生成带类型的构建器编辑，并附带明确的变更清单；Critic 则与门控层一起，拒绝那些其声称改进缺乏轨迹支撑的编辑，或那些接受会回退先前已解决任务的编辑。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              All stages share a single information substrate: the trace store, a structured record of execution events, verifier-scored outcomes, regression signals, and shipped or rejected edits. No stage consumes input beyond the trace store and the current harness Ht . Data flows forward through the pipeline with selective gating: the Digester may determine that no actionable failures exist (all tasks pass or signal is too sparse), terminating the round immediately; the Planner may find no viable adaptation landscape given the current evidence and edit history; and the Evolver may produce no type-safe candidates. In each case the round exits cleanly with a no-op outcome. Only the Critic and deterministic gate are unconditional: any candidate that survives the upstream stages must pass through both before shipping. The Critic may additionally issue a single revision request to the Evolver before returning its final verdict. 所有阶段共享单一信息载体：轨迹存储，一个结构化记录，包含执行事件、由验证器评分的结果、回归信号，以及已交付或被拒绝的编辑。任何阶段都不会超出轨迹存储和当前 harness Ht 之外消耗输入。数据沿流水线向前流动，并经过选择性门控：Digester 可能判定不存在可操作的失败（所有任务通过，或信号过于稀疏），从而立即终止该轮；Planner 在当前证据与编辑历史下可能找不到可行的适配局面；Evolver 也可能生成不了类型安全的候选。在每种情况下，该轮都会以无操作（no-op）结果干净退出。只有 Critic 与确定性门控是无条件的：任何在上游阶段存活的候选，在交付前都必须同时通过两者。Critic 还可能在给出最终裁决前，额外向 Evolver 发出一次单独的修订请求。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Digester. A single iteration on GAIA (103 tasks, pass@2) generates ∼10M tokens of raw traces: model reasoning steps, tool invocations with their outputs, and timing metadata. Passing this volume directly to downstream stages exceeds context limits, yet naive truncation discards diagnostic signal. The Digester compresses each task's traces into a structured per-task summary: binary outcome, failure category (if any), implicated component identifiers, and supporting evidence excerpts. It also provides cross-iteration continuity: each task's summary links to its history of prior outcomes and shipped edits, enabling the Planner to distinguish persistent failures from transient noise. Digester：对 GAIA（103 个任务，pass@2）进行一次迭代，会生成 ∼10M 个原始轨迹 token：模型推理步骤、带输出的工具调用，以及时序元数据。若直接把这份规模的内容送入下游阶段，会超出上下文限制，但盲目截断又会丢弃诊断信号。Digester 将每个任务的轨迹压缩为结构化的、逐任务的摘要：二元结果、失败类别（如有）、涉及的组件标识符，以及支撑证据片段。它还提供跨迭代的连续性：每个任务的摘要都会链接到其先前结果历史与已交付编辑的记录，使 Planner 能区分持续性失败与偶发噪声。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Planner. The Planner receives the Digester's output (task-level summaries enriched with cross-iteration history) and constructs an adaptation landscape: which tasks are failing, what edits have been attempted, which components are implicated, and which edit types (prompt, tool, processor, configuration) remain untried. This stage is the primary defense against under-exploration: by constructing the landscape before edit generation, it prevents the pipeline from converging on trace-conditional local repair, ensuring that structural changes (tool additions, processor rewrites, memory-policy redesigns) are considered alongside incremental prompt edits. Planner：Planner 接收 Digester 的输出（逐任务摘要，并融合跨迭代历史），并构建适配局面：哪些任务正在失败、尝试过哪些编辑、涉及哪些组件，以及哪些编辑类型（提示、工具、处理器、配置）尚未尝试。该阶段是对“探索不足”的主要防线：通过在生成编辑之前先构建局面，它能阻止流水线收敛到基于轨迹条件的局部修补，从而确保结构性变更（添加工具、重写处理器、重新设计内存策略）与增量提示编辑一并被纳入考虑。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Evolver. Given the Planner's adaptation landscape, the Evolver produces one or more candidate harnesses {H~tk}k=1Kt ,each specified as a typed builder operation on the current harness Ht . Each candidate carries a change mannifest: the edited components, the intended behavioral effect, and the tasks expected to improve or regress.
