                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              call. The environment replies with the next observation and the current 环境会返回下一条观察以及当前

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              admissible action list. 允许的动作列表。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              ##Output Discipline ##输出规范

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              • One act call per turn. No chaining ("go to fridge 1 and open it").

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              • 每回合只进行一次 act 调用。不要连用（例如“去冰箱1并把它打开”）。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              • Pick the command verbatim from the admissible list. Anything outside that list

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              • 从允许列表中逐字选择命令动词。只要不在该列表内，

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  is a silent no-op and wastes a step.     就会静默无效果（no-op），并浪费一步。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              • Reply with FINAL ANSWER: done only after you see ___ALFWORLD_DONE__ or

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              • 只有在观察中看到 ___ALFWORLD_DONE__ 或者

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  ___ALFWORLD_FAILED__ in an observation. Until then, keep calling act.     ___ALFWORLD_FAILED__ 后，才回复 FINAL ANSWER: done。在此之前继续调用 act。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              • If you genuinely cannot make progress for many consecutive steps, end with

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              • 如果你确实在许多连续步骤中都无法取得任何进展，就以

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  FINAL ANSWER: give up.     FINAL ANSWER: give up 结束。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              ##Task Types                                 ####任务类型

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Type| Goal| Key Steps |
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Pick & Place
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              take -> place |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              		$Y \mid$ Find $X \rightarrow$ take $X \rightarrow$ go to $Y \rightarrow$ put $X \mid$
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Y | Find X1 -> take -> place -> find X2 ->
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              desklamp || Examine in Light | Examine X under desklamp| Find X -> take X -> find desklamp -> use
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Clean & Place
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              go to $Y \rightarrow$ put |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              		| Find X -> take -> clean at sinkbasin ->
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Heat & Place
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              to $Y \rightarrow$ put |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              		Heat X and put in/on Y| Find X -> take -> heat at microwave -> go
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Cool & Place
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Y -> put |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              		| Cool X and put in/on Y| Find X -> take -> cool at fridge -> go to
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | 类型| 目标| 关键步骤 |
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | 取放
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              take -> place |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              		$Y \mid$ 找到 $X \rightarrow$ 取 $X \rightarrow$ 前往 $Y \rightarrow$ 放置 $X \mid$
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Y | 找到 X1 -> 取 -> 放置 -> 找到 X2 ->
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              台灯 || 光下检查 | 在台灯下检查 X| 找到 X -> 取 X -> 找到台灯 -> 使用
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | 清洁并放置
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              前往 $Y \rightarrow$ 放置 |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              		| 找到 X -> 取 -> 在水槽清洗 ->
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | 加热并放置
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              到 $Y \rightarrow$ 放置 |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              		加热 X 并放入/放在 Y 中/上| 找到 X -> 取 -> 在微波炉加热 -> 前往
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | 冷却并放置
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Y -> 放置 |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              		| 冷却 X 并放入/放在 Y 中/上| 找到 X -> 取 -> 在冰箱冷却 -> 前往

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              ##General Principles                                 ####一般原则

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              1. Decompose the goal into ordered sub-goals (locate -> acquire -> transform ->                                 1. 将目标分解为按顺序排列的子目标（定位 -> 获取 -> 转换 ->

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  deliver) and complete each before moving on.                                     送达），并在进入下一步前逐一完成。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              2. Systematic exploration: search each surface and container at most once before                                 2. 系统性探索：每个表面和容器在再次查看前最多搜索一次。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  revisiting. Open closed containers before judging them empty -- the                                     在判断封闭容器为空前先打开它们——

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  admissible list surfaces open <recep> when you arrive at a closed one.                                     到达封闭容器时，允许列表会显示 open <recep>。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              3. Grab immediately: when a required object appears, take it on the very next                                 3. 立即抓取：当所需物体出现时，在下一步就拿起它，

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  step before moving elsewhere.                                     再去别处移动。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              4. Transform before placing: perform any clean/heat/cool state change at the                                 4. 先转换再放置：在前往最终目的地前，先在

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  appropriate appliance before heading to the final destination.                                     相应设备上完成任何清洁/加热/冷却状态变化。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              5. Direct delivery: once holding the goal object, navigate straight to the                                 5. 直接送达：一旦拿到目标物体，就直接前往

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  target receptacle and place it.                                     目标容器并将其放入。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              6. Track progress: keep an internal count of objects still to find and place.                                 6. 跟踪进度：在内部记录仍需寻找和放置的物体数量。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  Only stop searching when the count reaches zero.                                     只有当数量降为零时才停止搜索。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              7. Avoid loops: never repeat the same action more than twice in a row. If stuck,                                 7. 避免循环：同一动作不要连续重复超过两次。若卡住，

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  move to a different unexplored location.                                     就移动到另一个未探索的位置。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              8. Trust the admissible list: if take X from Y does not appear, you are not at                                 8. 相信可接受列表：如果 take X from Y 不在其中，你就不能

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  Y,Y is closed,or X is not visible -- go to, open,or move on rather                                     Y,Y 是关闭的，或 X 不可见——应 go to、open，或转而继续

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  than guessing.                                     而不是猜测。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              ##Common Mistakes to Avoid                                 ####常见错误避免

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              - Revisiting searched locations without new evidence.                                 - - 在没有新证据的情况下重复查看已搜索过的位置。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              - Ignoring visible objects -- if the target appears, take it immediately.                                 - - 忽视可见物体——如果目标出现，立即拿取。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              - Skipping the state change -- do not place an object before cleaning / heating                                 - - 跳过状态变化——当任务要求时，不要在清洁 / 加热

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  / cooling it when the task requires it.                                     / 冷却之前就放置物体。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              - Premature termination -- do not reply FINAL ANSWER: done before the env                                 - - 过早结束——在环境发出 __ALFWORLD_DONE__ 之前，不要回复 FINAL ANSWER: done。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  emits __ALFWORLD_DONE__.                                     emits __ALFWORLD_DONE__。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              - Action loops -- repeatedly toggling or examining the same object wastes steps.                                 - - 动作循环——反复切换或检查同一物体会浪费步数。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              - Holding two objects at once -- you can only carry one. put the current one                                 - - 同时拿着两个物体——你一次只能携带一个。拿下一个之前，先 put 当前的。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  before take-ing the next.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              B.3 Change-Manifest Schema

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              B.3 变更清单架构

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Each Evolver candidate is accompanied by a change manifest, a structured audit record linking the proposed edit to its evidence, mechanism, expected effect, and attribution signal. The manifest makes every harness modification falsifiable: the Critic checks whether the next round's trace features match the mechanism and impact the manifest predicted. Table 9 defines the manifest fields, and the schema below specifies their representation. 每个 Evolver 候选都会附带一份变更清单：这是一条结构化的审计记录，将拟议的编辑与其证据、机制、预期效果以及归因信号关联起来。该清单使每一次 harness 修改都可证伪：审稿者会检查下一轮的轨迹特征是否符合清单所预测的机制与影响。表 9 定义了清单字段，以下架构则规定它们的表示方式。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Table 9 Change-manifest fields. The manifest is the loop's evidence ledger: every shipped edit is falsifiable against the next round's trace-feature deltas. 表 9 变更清单字段。该清单是循环的证据账本：每一项交付的编辑都可与下一轮的轨迹特征增量进行证伪对照。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              FieldMeaning
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              candidate idUnique id, e.g. C-R3-01 (round 3, candidate 1).
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              bucketEdit type: prompt, tools, config, or processor.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              capability evidenceVerified claims that the edit mechanism actually works.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              file changesList of path / action / diff-summary edits.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              predicted impactTasks the edit will unlock, stabilize, or put at risk: the falsifiable prediction.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              attribution signatureTrace feature that must appear if the edit fired, e.g. a processor invocation.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              字段含义
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              候选项 id唯一 id，例如 C-R3-01（第 3 轮，第 1 个候选项）。
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              桶编辑类型：提示、工具、配置或处理器。
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              能力证据经验证的声明，表明该编辑机制确实有效。
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              文件变更路径 / 操作 / 差异摘要的编辑列表。
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              预测影响该编辑将解锁、稳定或带来风险的任务：可证伪的预测。
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              归因签名如果该编辑生效，必须出现的可追踪特征，例如一次处理器调用。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              change_manifest.yaml (schema) change_manifest.yaml（schema）

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              candidate_id <round/candidate_id> candidate_id <round/candidate_id>

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              bucket prompt | tools | config | processor bucket 提示词 | 工具 | 配置 | 处理器

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              capability_evidence capability_evidence

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              • {type: python_package | filesystem | other, claim: "...", evidence: "..."}

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              • {type: python_package | filesystem | other, claim: "...", evidence: "..."}

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              file_changes file_changes

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              • {path: "...", action: create | modify | delete, diff_summary: "..."}

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              • {path: "...", action: create | modify | delete, diff_summary: "..."}

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              predicted_impact predicted_impact

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              tasks_will_unlock [task_id, ...] tasks_will_unlock [task_id, ...]

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              tasks_will_stabilize [task_id, ...] tasks_will_stabilize [task_id, ...]

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              tasks_at_risk [task_id, ...] tasks_at_risk [task_id, ...]

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              attribution_signature attribution_signature

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              type processor_invocation | tool_call | prompt_feature type processor_invocation | tool_call | prompt_feature

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              tool_name tool_name

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              expected_min_calls expected_min_calls

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              C Anatomy of an Evolution Step

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              C 演化步骤剖析

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              To illustrate the AEGIS loop concretely, we walk through one full cycle, from Digester compression through Planner synthesis, Evolver editing, and Critic judgment, to the resulting trace delta. We select round 10 of the GAIA / Sonnet 4.6 run: a composite edit spanning a new tool, a prompt addition, and a configuration change. This multi-component intervention produced the largest single-round gain in that run, making it a richer illustration than a single-lever edit. 为具体说明 AEGIS 循环，我们完整走一遍：从 Digester 压缩，到 Planner 合成、Evolver 编辑，再到 Critic 判断，直至得到 trace 增量。我们选取 GAIA / Sonnet 4.6 运行的第 10 轮：一次复合编辑，涵盖一个新工具、一项提示词追加和一次配置变更。这个多组件干预带来了该次运行中单轮最大的收益，因此比单一杠杆式编辑更能说明问题。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              C.1 Worked Example: GAIA / Sonnet 4.6, Round 10

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              C.1 工作示例：GAIA / Sonnet 4.6，第10轮

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Failure evidence. Prior to round 10,the success rate stood at 74.8%, having dropped from its peak of 77.7% due to a regression in round 9. The Digester's trace analysis revealed a systematic failure pattern: every Wikipedia fetch in round 10's traces returned zero characters. WebFetch employs a browser if the website requires JavaScript support, but Wikipedia's new frontend fails to load correctly, timing out or returning an empty body. The traces make it plain: within task db4fd70a (number of stations in a rail line), db4fd70a_r0.jsonl#step_0 and #step_1 report that Wikipedia WebFetch fetches return 0 chars; similarly, within f0f46385 (ASEAN members' membership status), three consecutive WebFetch calls return 0 chars; ten separate attempts across the round returned empty responses. 失败证据。在第10轮之前，成功率为74.8%，因第9轮的回归而从77.7%的峰值下降。Digester 的轨迹分析揭示了一个系统性失败模式：第10轮轨迹中的每一次 Wikipedia 抓取都返回了零字符。WebFetch 在网站需要 JavaScript 支持时会使用浏览器，但 Wikipedia 的新前端无法正确加载，导致超时或返回空主体。轨迹清楚表明：在任务 db4fd70a（铁路线上的车站数量）中，db4fd70a_r0.jsonl#step_0 和 #step_1 报告 Wikipedia WebFetch 抓取返回 0 字符；同样，在 f0f46385（东盟成员国的成员资格状态）中，连续三次 WebFetch 调用都返回 0 字符；该轮共有十次独立尝试返回空响应。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Planner synthesis. The Digester grouped the 23 failed tasks by failure mode, surfacing a critical tool-level issue that the round-9 Critic had already flagged: the tools component had not shipped a fix in nine consecutive rounds despite source-access failures appearing since round 1. The Planner received two targets: (1) resolve the persistent tool-level source-access failures, and (2) revert the prompt and budget-processor changes responsible for the round-9 regression. Planner 综合。Digester 按失败模式对 23 个失败任务进行了分组，暴露出一个关键的工具层问题：第9轮 Critic 早已指出，尽管自第1轮起就出现源访问失败，tools 组件却连续九轮都未交付修复。Planner 收到两个目标：(1) 解决持续存在的工具层源访问失败；(2) 回滚导致第9轮回归的 prompt 和 budget-processor 更改。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Evolver edit. The Evolver suggested C-R10-02, covering three buckets. (i) tools: new WikiTextFetch tool, avoiding the browser altogether by employing the MediaWiki API endpoint; returns complete text of the article, 10,529 chars in case of the rail line, 80,028 for ASEAN. (ii) prompt: a single sentence in the tool usage section instructing the agent to use WikiTextFetch before looking up Wikipedia articles. (iii) config: restore the round-8 baseline configuration, register WikiTextFetch, and remove the problematic budget processor. The manifest's capability evidence includes a Level-2 round-trip check (content serialized as a 10,529-char string by the provider); the attribution signature requires at least one WikiTextFetch call. Evolver 编辑。Evolver 提议了 C-R10-02，涵盖三类变更。(i) tools：新增 WikiTextFetch 工具，完全绕过浏览器，改用 MediaWiki API 端点；返回文章完整文本，在铁路线案例中为 10,529 字符，在 ASEAN 案例中为 80,028 字符。(ii) prompt：在工具使用部分加入一句话，指示代理在查找 Wikipedia 文章前先使用 WikiTextFetch。(iii) config：恢复第8轮基线配置，注册 WikiTextFetch，并移除有问题的 budget processor。清单的能力证据包含一次 Level-2 往返检查（内容由提供方序列化为 10,529 字符串）；归因签名要求至少一次 WikiTextFetch 调用。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              R10/candidates/C-R10-02.md R10/candidates/C-R10-02.md

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              candidate_id C-R10-02 candidate_id C-R10-02

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              bucket [tools, prompt, config] bucket [tools, prompt, config]

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              capability_evidence capability_evidence

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  - type: http_endpoint     - - type: http_endpoint

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      claim "MediaWiki API returns full plain-text extract where WebFetch returns 0 chars"         claim "MediaWiki API returns full plain-text extract where WebFetch returns 0 chars"

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      evidence "GET .../w/api.php?...&explaintext=true -> 10,529 chars for Franklin/Foxboro_Line"         evidence "GET .../w/api.php?...&explaintext=true -> 10,529 chars for Franklin/Foxboro_Line"

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  - type: other     - - type: other

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      claim "tool return survives provider serialization to the model (Level 2)"         claim "tool return survives provider serialization to the model (Level 2)"

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      evidence "prepare_messages([tool_msg]) keeps content as 10,529-char string"         evidence "prepare_messages([tool_msg]) keeps content as 10,529-char string"

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              file_changes file_changes

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  - {path: R10/applied/C-R10-02/wiki_text_fetch.py, action: create, diff_summary: "WikiTextFetch via MediaWiki API"}     - - {path: R10/applied/C-R10-02/wiki_text_fetch.py, action: create, diff_summary: "通过 MediaWiki API 的 WikiTextFetch"}

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  - {path: R10/applied/C-R10-02/gaia_agent.md, action: create, diff_summary: "R8 prompt + one WikiTextFetch line"}     - - {path: R10/applied/C-R10-02/gaia_agent.md, action: create, diff_summary: "R8 提示 + 一行 WikiTextFetch"}

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  - {path: R10/applied/C-R10-02/config.yaml, action: create, diff_summary: "register tool; restore R8; drop budget     - - {path: R10/applied/C-R10-02/config.yaml, action: create, diff_summary: "注册工具；恢复 R8；移除预算\n

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          processor"}             processor"}

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              predicted_impact predicted_impact

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  tasks_will_unlock [db4fd70a, f0f46385, 983bba7c, 08f3a05f, 5e2a91b0]     tasks_will_unlock [db4fd70a, f0f46385, 983bba7c, 08f3a05f, 5e2a91b0]

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  tasks_will_stabilize [4b6bb5f7, 42d4198c]     tasks_will_stabilize [4b6bb5f7, 42d4198c]

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  tasks_at_risk []     tasks_at_risk []

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              attribution_signature attribution_signature

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  type tool_call     type tool_call

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  tool_name WikiTextFetch     tool_name WikiTextFetch

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  expected_min_calls 1     expected_min_calls 1

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Critic verdict. The Critic approved only C-R10-02, rejecting the competing revert-only candidate C-R10-01. Its rationale covered three aspects. (i) Interaction: C-R10-02 is a strict superset of C-R10-01; both restore the round-8 baseline, so the budget-processor removal is intentional, not an accidental overlap. (ii) Round-trip evidence: the Critic verified Level-2 evidence (tool output arrives as a full string, not a truncation marker) before accepting any tools-bucket candidate. (iii) Portfolio: this is the first tools-bucket ship in ten rounds, and the trace evidence of persistent zero-char returns justifies the intervention that round-9 flagged. Critic 判定。Critic 仅批准了 C-R10-02，拒绝了竞争性的“仅回退”候选 C-R10-01。其理由涵盖三点。（i）交互：C-R10-02 是 C-R10-01 的严格超集；两者都恢复了回合 8 的基线，因此移除 budget-processor 是有意为之，而不是不小心的重叠。（ii）往返证据：在接受任何 tools-bucket 候选之前，Critic 在验证关卡 2 证据（工具输出以完整字符串返回，而不是截断标记）。（iii）组合：这是十个回合里首次出现 tools-bucket 船，而回溯证据表明回合 9 标记的“持续零字符返回”确实存在，因此证明了当时的干预是合理的。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Delta realized. Post-shipping, the GAIA pass rate increased from 74.8% at R9 to 79.6% at R10 (+4.9pp, five tasks changed to pass), the greatest improvement during the entire run; five of the seven tasks the tool was predicted to affect flipped to pass (hit rate 0.71, the highest for any ship across all 19 runs). The improvement mainly occurred at Levels 2 (+4 tasks) and 3 (+2). Since the tool triggered its target tasks, the attribution condition was satisfied. Delta 实现。交付后，GAIA 通过率从 R9 的 74.8% 提升到 R10 的 79.6%（+4.9pp，5 个任务从失败变为通过），这是整个运行中提升幅度最大的结果。被预测会受到该工具影响的 7 个任务中有 5 个翻转为通过（命中率 0.71，也是所有 19 轮中最高）。提升主要发生在 Level 2（+4 个任务）和 Level 3（+2）。由于该工具触发了目标任务，归因条件得到满足。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Figure 7 summarizes the same edit as a manifest card: the raw YAML above is what the loop logs, the card is the human-facing reading of it. 图 7 用清单卡的形式总结了同一处编辑：上方那段原始 YAML 就是循环记录的内容，而这张卡则是面向人类的可读版本。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              D Additional Results

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              D 额外结果

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              The rest of this appendix is organized per benchmark. Each subsection is built around one figure of three panels: (a) a breakdown of the failure clusters the adaptation loop had to address, (b) the per-model distribution of harness levers the evolution shipped, and (c) a model-by-lever heatmap of each lever's effectiveness (tasks flipped to pass over tasks predicted). We read the three panels in order: what fails and why, how each model evolves, and whether the evolution closes the failures. 本附录其余内容按基准逐一组织。每个小节围绕一张包含三联图的图展开：（a）适应循环需要处理的失败簇拆解，（b）进化为每个模型交付的运筹杠杆分布，以及（c）各杠杆效力的模型-杠杆热力图（将任务预测为能通过的任务翻转以验证）。我们依次阅读三联图：失败是什么、原因是什么，各模型如何演化，以及演化是否能终结这些失败。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              D.1 GAIA

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              D.1 GAIA

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              GAIA stresses general reasoning under tool use, and is the most lever-diverse benchmark in our suite. Figure 8 gives the three views we use throughout this appendix. GAIA 强调在使用工具时的通用推理能力，是我们套件中杠杆种类最丰富的基准。图 8 给出了本附录中我们使用的三种视角。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Failure clusters and their causes. Panel (a) summarizes the failure clusters accumulated across the GAIA run. The dominant cluster is blocked-source (39%), where the agent cannot retrieve the required evidence because pages return empty content, require JavaScript rendering that times out, or contain incomplete information. Reasoning failures (33%) follow, covering tasks that require multi-hop inference, disambiguation 失败集及其原因。（a）面板汇总了 GAIA 运行期间累计的失败集。主要失败集是被阻断的源（39%）：智能体无法检索所需证据，因为页面返回空内容，需要 JavaScript 渲染但会超时，或包含不完整信息。推理失败（33%）紧随其后，覆盖需要多跳推断、消歧

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              C-R10-02 | GAIA / Sonnet 4.6 | round 10 | bucket: tools+prompt+config C-R10-02 | GAIA / Sonnet 4.6 | 第 10 轮 | bucket: tools+prompt+config

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Failure evidence 失败证据

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Every Wikipedia WebFetch in the round's trajectories returns 0 chars (10+ calls across db4fd70a, fof46385, 42d4198c); the headless browser fallback times out on Wikipedia's frontend. 本轮轨迹中的每次 Wikipedia WebFetch 都返回 0 个字符（db4fd70a、fof46385、42d4198c 共 10+ 次调用）；在 Wikipedia 前端上，后备的无头浏览器会超时。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Edit 编辑

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              New WikiTextFetch tool calls the MediaWiki API for plain-text article content (tools); one prompt line routes Wikipedia lookups to it first (prompt); the round-8 baseline is restored and the regressing budget processor removed (config). 新的 WikiTextFetch 工具调用 MediaWiki API 获取纯文本文章内容（tools）；第一行提示将维基百科查询先路由到它（prompt）；恢复第 8 轮基线，并移除了会回归的预算处理器（config）。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Predicted fixes 预测修复

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Unlock db4fd70a, f0f46385, 983bba7c, 08f3a05f, 5e2a91b0; stabilize 4b6bb5f7, 42d4198c. 解锁 db4fd70a、f0f46385、983bba7c、08f3a05f、5e2a91b0；稳定 4b6bb5f7、42d4198c。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Attribution signature 归因签名

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              tool_call on WikiTextFetch, ≥ 1 call; verified against the next round's trace. 对 WikiTextFetch 的 tool_call，≥ 1 次；已与下一轮的追踪记录核验。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Figure 7 Change manifest for C-R10-02 rendered as a manifest card, the human-facing counterpart of the logged YAML above. 图 7：针对 C-R10-02 的变更清单被渲染成清单卡片，即上方记录的 YAML 的面向人工的对应物。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              of similar entities, numerical computation, or precise interpretation of underspecified queries. Figure/visual failures (11%) arise when the answer depends on information embedded in images, maps, or diagrams that text extraction alone cannot capture. Document/table parsing failures (11%) occur when evidence is locked in PDFs, structured tables, or semi-structured formats and the agent either misparses the layout or overlooks relevant cells. Scope ambiguity (6%) covers queries with multiple valid interpretations, where the agent answers a related but incorrect reading. Together, these clusters indicate that GAIA failures concentrate in evidence retrieval, multi-step reasoning, visual grounding, structured-document extraction, and query disambiguation. 以及类似实体、数值计算，或对信息不足查询的精确解读。当答案依赖于嵌入在图片、地图或图表中的信息，而仅靠文本抽取无法捕获时，就会出现图/视觉失败（11%）。当证据被锁在 PDF、结构化表格或半结构化格式中，且智能体要么误解析版式、要么忽略相关单元格时，会发生文档/表格解析失败（11%）。范围歧义（6%）涵盖对存在多种有效理解的查询：智能体给出的是相关但不正确的解读。合起来看，这些失败表明 GAIA 的故障主要集中在证据检索、多步推理、视觉落地、结构化文档抽取以及查询消歧上。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Per-model evolution logic. Panel (b) shows GAIA is the only benchmark where all four levers see substantial use; the Sonnet run alone shipped 11 prompt, 7 processor, 7 config, and 6 tools edits, because its failure set spans tool, prompt, and config problems simultaneously. The three models nonetheless diverge in a way that tracks their starting competence: Sonnet, with the most rounds, sweeps every lever; GPT-5.4 leans hardest on prompt (45% of its ships ) and barely touches config,since its reasoning is already strong enough that the remaining gains are mostly instruction-following; Qwen3.5's short run concentrates its few ships and, strikingly, lands its single tools ship at the highest yield of any cell. The shared logic is prompt-first for the behavioral clusters, with the scarce tools lever reserved for the one mechanical cluster prose cannot touch. 按模型的演化逻辑。（b）面板显示：GAIA 是唯一一个四个杠杆都被显著使用的基准；Sonnet 的运行单独就提交了 11 次提示、7 次处理器、7 次配置和 6 次工具编辑，因为它的失败集合同时覆盖了工具、提示和配置的问题。尽管如此，三个模型在某种程度上仍按其起始能力出现分歧：Sonnet（轮次最多）会遍历每个杠杆；GPT-5.4 最依赖提示 (45% of its ships )，几乎不触及 config，因为它的推理已足够强，剩余增益大多来自指令遵循；Qwen3.5 的短运行集中在它少量的几次提交，而且尤其突出的是，它那唯一一次 tools 提交的收益是该表中所有单元格里最高的。共享逻辑是：行为集群上的提示优先；稀缺的 tools 杠杆仅留给那些机械层面的集群，而这些集群的文字描述无法触及。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Did evolution close the clusters? Panel (c) shows which failure clusters were reduced by evolution. The largest improvement comes from the blocked-source cluster: the tool edit that introduced WikiTextFetch replaced unreliable browser-based Wikipedia fetching with a MediaWiki API call, reducing failures caused by empty or incomplete page retrieval. Prompt edits mainly targeted the reasoning cluster by encouraging more explicit verification, which contributed to steady gains across rounds. By contrast, figure / visual and document / table parsing failures remained harder to reduce, because they require information extraction from images, figures, PDFs, or structured tables. Overall, GAIA improves through a combination of tool edits that fix retrieval failures and prompt edits that reduce reasoning errors, while residual errors concentrate in visual and document-heavy tasks. 演化是否关闭了这些集群？（c）面板展示了哪些失败集群被演化所减少。最大的改进来自被阻断源的失败集：引入 WikiTextFetch 的那次工具编辑，用一次 MediaWiki API 调用取代了不可靠的基于浏览器的维基百科抓取，从而降低了由空内容或不完整页面检索引发的失败。提示编辑主要通过鼓励更明确的核验来瞄准推理集群，这带来了各轮持续的收益。相反，图/视觉以及文档/表格解析失败仍更难以降低，因为它们需要从图片、图表、PDF 或结构化表格中抽取信息。总体而言，GAIA 通过两类组合来改进：修复检索失败的工具编辑，以及减少推理错误的提示编辑；剩余错误则主要集中在视觉与文档负载更高的任务中。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              D.2 ALFWorld

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              D.2 ALFWorld

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              ALFWorld is an embodied planning benchmark and the most prompt-dominated in our suite. Figure 9 shows its clusters, per-model logic, and effectiveness. ALFWorld 是一种具身规划基准，也是我们套件中最“依赖提示词”的基准。图 9 展示了它的聚类、按模型的逻辑及其有效性。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Figure 8 GAIA evolution analysis (103 tasks, exact-match). (a) Failure clusters among the tasks still unsolved; blocked-source and reasoning dominate, while figure/visual and parsing clusters are residual model gaps. (b) Share of shipped edits by bucket for each task model. (c) Lever effectiveness as hit-rate (tasks flipped / predicted) per model and bucket. The single Qwen3.5 tools ship is the highest-yield cell (0.67). 图 8 GAIA 演化分析（103 个任务，精确匹配）。(a) 仍未解决任务中的失败聚类；被阻塞的来源与推理占主导，而图像/视觉与解析聚类则是残余的模型缺口。(b) 各任务模型按桶统计已发布编辑的占比。(c) 以命中率（翻转任务/预测任务）衡量的提示词有效性，按模型和桶统计。唯一一项 Qwen3.5 tools 的发布单元产出最高（0.67）。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Failure clusters and their causes. Panel (a) summarizes the main failure clusters observed on ALFWorld. The dominant cluster is search / step-ceiling (89%), which covers episodes where the agent either searches rooms or receptacles in an inefficient order, or reaches the step limit before completing long interaction chains such as deep search or transform-then-place tasks. Prompt-rule side-effect failures (7%) occur when an added heuristic improves some tasks but unintentionally restricts behavior on others, causing the agent to skip a valid action or stop searching too early. Object-type confusion failures (4%) refer to cases where the agent confuses semantically similar objects. Together, these clusters show that ALFWorld failures mainly arise from search efficiency, over-constrained prompting, and object-specific grounding errors. 失败聚类及其原因。（a）总结了在 ALFWorld 上观察到的主要失败聚类。主导聚类是搜索 / 步数上限（89%）：涵盖了智能体要么以低效顺序搜索房间或容器，要么在完成诸如深度搜索或先变换再放置等长交互链之前就触及步数限制。提示规则带来的副作用失败（7%）发生在加入某个启发式能改善部分任务，却在其他任务上意外限制了行为，导致智能体跳过一个有效动作或过早停止搜索。对象类型混淆失败（4%）指智能体把在语义上相近的对象搞混的情况。综合来看，这些聚类表明 ALFWorld 的失败主要源于搜索效率、过度约束的提示，以及对象特定的定位错误。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Per-model evolution logic. Panel (b) shows that prompt dominance scales inversely with base-model strength: a prompt rule yields gains only for models that reliably follow it. Sonnet, being the strongest base, derives nearly all improvement from prompt edits alone; search-order heuristics in the system prompt suffice because it consistently obeys them. GPT-5.4 supplements prompts with a processor (introduced at round three) that manages its step budget for transformation tasks. Qwen3.5 requires the most varied mix (prompt, config, and processor), including a processor that intercepts its reasoning text and re-emits tool calls when needed, a mechanical fix for a failure that prompt-level steering cannot resolve. The shared pattern is prompt-first, with structural levers recruited only when prompts prove insufficient. The weaker the base model, the sooner evolution falls back from prompt-based steering to config or processor enforcement, visible in the growing non-prompt segments from Sonnet to Qwen. 按模型的演化逻辑。（b）显示：提示主导程度与基础模型强度呈反比——只有那些能可靠遵循提示规则的模型，提示规则才会带来收益。由于 Sonnet 是最强的基础模型，它几乎全部改进都来自提示编辑本身；系统提示里的搜索顺序启发式就足够了，因为它能持续遵循。GPT-5.4 在第三轮引入的处理器（processor）中用其补全提示：该处理器为变换类任务管理步预算。Qwen3.5 需要最多元的组合（提示、配置和处理器），其中包含一个拦截其推理文本并在需要时重新发出工具调用的处理器——这是提示级别引导无法解决的机械性修复。共同模式是“先提示”，只有在提示被证明不足时才调动结构性杠杆。基础模型越弱，演化越快从基于提示的引导回退到配置或处理器的强制约束，这可从 Sonnet 到 Qwen 的非提示部分不断增长中看出。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Did evolution close the clusters? Panel (c) shows that evolution reduced the main ALFWorld failure clusters, with different levers mattering for different task agents. For Qwen3.5, processor and config edits achieved the strongest effects, with hit-rates of 0.84 and 0.71 , respectively. These edits directly addressed mechanical failures by re-emitting missed tool calls and adjusting execution budgets, allowing Qwen3.5 to improve by +44.0pp and approach the closed-model runs. For Sonnet, the remaining failures were less structural, so prompt edits were sufficient for most gains, reaching a 0.49 hit-rate, while the processor edit had only a marginal effect (0.14). Two clusters were only partially reduced: prompt-rule side effects, which were introduced by some evolved heuristics and then patched in later rounds, and long-path failures, where some episodes still exceeded the available interaction budget. Overall, ALFWorld shows a clear model-dependent pattern: stronger models benefit mainly from prompt-level steering, whereas weaker models require more structural support through processor and configuration edits. 演化是否关闭了这些聚类？（c）表明，演化减少了主要的 ALFWorld 失败聚类，不同任务智能体依赖的杠杆也不同。对 Qwen3.5 而言，处理器与配置编辑带来了最强效果，命中率分别为 0.84 和 0.71。这些编辑通过重新发出遗漏的工具调用并调整执行预算，直接修复了机械性失败，使 Qwen3.5 提升了 +44.0pp，并接近“已关闭模型”的运行结果。对 Sonnet 来说，剩余失败的结构性较弱，因此提示编辑就足以带来大部分收益，命中率达到 0.49，而处理器编辑的影响只有很小的幅度（0.14）。只有两个聚类被部分减少：提示规则副作用——由部分进化出的启发式引入，随后在后续轮次中被修补；以及长路径失败——仍有一些样本超出了可用的交互预算。总体而言，ALFWorld 呈现出清晰的模型依赖模式：更强的模型主要受益于提示级别引导，而更弱的模型则需要通过处理器与配置编辑提供更多结构性支持。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Figure 9 ALFWorld evolution analysis (134 tasks, goal-completion). (a) Failure clusters accumulated across all rounds; search inefficiency and the hard step-ceiling dominate, with two small clusters that evolution itself introduced (a prompt-rule side-effect) or transiently hit (object-type confusion). (b) Lever mix by model: the strong base (Sonnet) climbs on prompt almost alone, while weaker bases reach for more varied levers. (c) Lever effectiveness: structural levers (processor, config) are both used more and more effective on weaker models. 图 9 ALFWorld 演化分析（134 个任务，目标完成）。(a) 所有轮次累计的失败聚类；搜索低效与“困难的步数上限”占主导，并且有两个小聚类是由演化本身引入（提示规则副作用）或被短暂触发（对象类型混淆）。(b) 按模型的杠杆组合：强基础（Sonnet）几乎全靠提示就能攀升，而较弱的基础会尝试更多元的杠杆。(c) 杠杆有效性：结构性杠杆（处理器、配置）在弱模型上既被使用得更多，也同样更有效。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              D.3 WebShop

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              D.3 WebShop

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              WebShop is a web-interaction benchmark and the noisiest run in our suite. Figure 10 shows its clusters, per-model logic, and effectiveness. WebShop 是一个网页交互基准，也是我们套件中噪声最大的一次运行。图 10 展示了其聚类、各模型逻辑和有效性。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Failure clusters and their causes. Panel (a) summarizes the WebShop failure clusters accumulated across the run. Early failures are dominated by search and pagination loops, where the agent repeatedly reformulates queries or cycles through result pages without committing to a purchase. As evolution reduces these control-flow errors, the remaining failures shift toward product-selection judgment. The largest cluster, wrong product (46%), occurs when the agent selects an item from the wrong category or settles on a weak match before comparing alternatives. Pagination loop failures (21%) capture the remaining cases of repeated next/previous navigation without progress. Colour matching failures (17%) arise when the agent mishandles shade equivalence or site-specific color labels, such as treating "wine" and "red" as incompatible. Attribute check failures (17%) occur when the selected item is close to the request but fails on a required detail, such as size, sleeve length, or another unverified attribute. Overall, the cluster shift indicates that evolution first reduces navigation loops, after which the main errors concentrate in product matching and attribute verification. 失败聚类及其成因。面板 (a) 总结了整个运行过程中累积的 WebShop 失败聚类。早期失败主要是搜索和分页循环，智能体反复改写查询或在结果页之间来回切换，却不提交购买。随着演化减少这些控制流错误，剩余失败转向商品选择判断。最大聚类“选错商品”(46%) 出现在智能体从错误类别中选取物品，或在比较备选项之前就接受了一个较弱的匹配结果。分页循环失败(21%) 记录了反复下一页/上一页导航而无进展的其余情况。颜色匹配失败(17%) 源于智能体处理色调等价或站点特定颜色标签不当，例如将“wine”和“red”视为不兼容。属性检查失败(17%) 出现在所选商品与需求接近，但在某个必需细节上不符合，例如尺寸、袖长或其他未核验属性。总体来看，聚类变化表明演化首先减少了导航循环，随后主要错误集中到商品匹配和属性验证上。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Per-model evolution logic. Panel (b) shows that prompt edits drive most of the improvement across all three models, with processor edits serving as a consistent secondary lever. This pattern matches WebShop's main control-flow failures. Prompt rules help the agent search more efficiently and commit earlier, while advisory processors reinforce these rules during execution by adding warnings when the agent begins to repeat searches or cycle through pagination. For product-selection failures, evolution introduces more targeted support: a colour-matching tool helps resolve shade-equivalence cases, and config edits help weaker models maintain context over longer shopping sessions. Overall, WebShop requires a mixed response: prompts improve high-level shopping strategy, processors reduce navigation loops, tools support attribute matching, and config edits stabilize long-session behavior. 各模型的演化逻辑。面板 (b) 显示，提示词编辑推动了三种模型中大部分改进，而处理器编辑则作为稳定的次要手段。这一模式与 WebShop 的主要控制流失败相符。提示规则帮助智能体更高效地搜索并更早做出决策，而辅助处理器则在执行过程中通过在智能体开始重复搜索或在分页间循环时添加警告来强化这些规则。对于商品选择失败，演化引入了更有针对性的支持：颜色匹配工具帮助处理色调等价问题，配置编辑帮助较弱模型在更长的购物会话中保持上下文。总体而言，WebShop 需要混合响应：提示词改善高层购物策略，处理器减少导航循环，工具支持属性匹配，配置编辑稳定长会话行为。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Did evolution close the clusters? Panel (c) shows that evolution partially reduced the WebShop failure clusters. Prompt edits were the most consistently effective lever across models, with hit-rates of 0.37-0.50, while config edits helped the two weaker models maintain context over longer sessions. These changes reduced early search and pagination loops, raising performance from 60% to a peak of 76%. The remaining clusters proved harder to close. Advisory processors produced only modest gains (0.20-0.25), so some pagination failures persisted. The colour-matching tool did not improve performance in this run (0.0 hit-rate), leaving the colour-matching cluster largely unchanged. Overall, WebShop benefits most from prompt and config edits, while residual navigation loops and product-judgment errors remain the main sources of instability. 演化是否关闭了这些聚类？面板 (c) 显示，演化部分削弱了 WebShop 的失败聚类。提示词编辑是跨模型最稳定有效的手段，命中率为 0.37-0.50，而配置编辑帮助两个较弱模型在更长会话中保持上下文。这些变化减少了早期搜索和分页循环，将性能从 60% 提升至 76% 的峰值。剩余聚类更难消除。辅助处理器只带来了有限收益(0.20-0.25)，因此一些分页失败仍然存在。颜色匹配工具在本次运行中未提升性能(0.0 命中率)，使颜色匹配聚类基本未变。总体而言，WebShop 最受益于提示词和配置编辑，而残余的导航循环和商品判断错误仍是主要不稳定来源。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Figure 10 WebShop evolution analysis (100 sessions). (a) Failure clusters across the run, after evolution has tamed the round-0 search/pagination loops; the residual is mostly product-choice judgment (wrong product, colour matching, attribute check). (b) Lever mix by model: prompt carries the climb, processor is the consistent second lever. (c) Lever effectiveness; prompt and config are the productive levers, the lone colour-matcher tool ship returned 0.0. 图 10 WebShop 演化分析（100 次会话）。(a) 全程失败聚类；在演化驯服了第 0 轮的搜索/分页循环后，残余主要是商品选择判断（选错商品、颜色匹配、属性检查）。(b) 各模型的杠杆组合：提示词带动上升，处理器是稳定的第二杠杆。(c) 杠杆有效性；提示词和配置是有效杠杆，唯一的颜色匹配工具 ship 返回了 0.0。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              D. τ3 -Bench

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              D. τ3 -Bench

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              τ3 -Bench stresses multi-turn dialogue under an explicit domain policy. Figure 11 pools the AEGIS runs across the airline, retail, and telecom domains. τ3 -Bench 在明确的领域政策下对多轮对话进行压力测试。图 11 将 AEGIS 在航空、零售和电信领域的运行结果汇总。

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Failure clusters and their causes. Excluding harness-interrupt traces, the failures are judgment-heavy: the top two clusters, premature / unverified action (28%; committing a booking, refund, or device fix before a precondition holds) and wrong selection / count (24%), together exceed half and concern when to commit and what to pick rather than mechanical execution. The remainder is procedural (incomplete multi-step fix 16%, missed step / sub-task 14%) or policy-related (misinterpretation 13%). The smallest cluster, capability-boundary confusion (5%),is τ3 -specific: some telecom faults live on the user’s handset,where the agent has no device-side tool and the failure is its treating that boundary as a missing capability. 失败簇及其原因。剔除 harness-interrupt 轨迹后，失败主要是判断型：最大的两个簇是过早 / 未验证的操作（28%；在前置条件成立前就做出承诺，例如完成订票、退款或设备修复）和错误选择 / 计数（24%）；二者合计超过一半，关注的是何时提交、选择什么，而非机械执行。其余为流程型（不完整的多步骤修复 16%、遗漏步骤 / 子任务 14%）或与政策相关的（误解 13%）。最小的簇是能力边界混淆（5%），这是 τ3 特有的：部分电信故障发生在用户手持设备上，而智能体没有设备侧工具，因而把这一边界当成了缺失能力。
